name: Magic Item Rarity, Values, and Acquisition
description: Rules for magic item rarity, values, awarding items to characters, activation mechanics, and crafting

magic_item_rarity.title: Magic Item Rarity
magic_item_rarity.overview: Every magic item has a rarity, which provides a rough measure of an item's power relative to other magic items
magic_item_rarity.rarity_spectrum: Common magic items, such as a Potion of Healing, are the most plentiful. Artifacts, such as the Orb of Dragonkind, are priceless, unique, and difficult to acquire

magic_item_values.title: Magic Item Values by Rarity
magic_item_values.overview: Common magic items can often be bought in a town or city. Uncommon and Rare magic items are usually found only in cities, and rarer magic items might be sold only in wondrous locations
magic_item_values.usage: If you allow characters to buy and sell magic items in your campaign, rarity can help you set prices for those items
notes: Halve the value for a consumable item other than a Spell Scroll
The value of a Spell Scroll is double what it costs to scribe the scroll

base_item_cost.description: If a magic item incorporates an item that has a purchase cost in Equipment (such as a weapon or a suit of armor), add that item's cost to the magic item's value
base_item_cost.example: +1 Armor (Plate Armor) has a value of 5,500 GP, which is the sum of a Rare magic item's value (4,000 GP) and the cost of Plate Armor (1,500 GP)

awarding_magic_items.title: Awarding Magic Items
awarding_magic_items.overview: Awarding magic items is the purview of the DM. You can award a magic item because the story calls for it or the players would be especially pleased to have it

are_magic_items_necessary.title: Are Magic Items Necessary?
are_magic_items_necessary.premise: The D&D game assumes that magic items appear sporadically and that they are a boon unless an item bears a curse
are_magic_items_necessary.design: Characters and monsters are built to face each other without the help of magic items, which means that having a magic item makes a character more powerful or versatile than a generic character of the same level
are_magic_items_necessary.dm_perspective: As DM, you never have to worry about awarding magic items just so the characters can keep up with the campaign's threats. Magic items are truly prizes—desirable but not necessary

magic_items_awarded_by_level.title: Magic Items Awarded by Level
magic_items_awarded_by_level.overview: The Magic Items Awarded by Level table shows the number of magic items a D&D party typically gains during a campaign, totaling one hundred magic items by level 20
magic_items_awarded_by_level.structure: The table shows how many items of each rarity are meant to be handed out during each of the four tiers of play
magic_items_awarded_by_level.artifacts: Artifacts are omitted from the table because they are most often used as plot devices in high-level adventures, and characters rarely have them for long (either because the Artifacts are meant to be destroyed or because the campaign is nearing its end)

player_wish_list.title: Player Wish List
player_wish_list.recommendation: Encourage your players to keep a wish list of magic items they hope their characters will find in the course of the campaign
player_wish_list.usage: If you want to award a magic item but don't have a specific magic item in mind, you can pick an item of the appropriate rarity from your players' wish list

overstocking_an_adventure.title: Overstocking an Adventure
overstocking_an_adventure.guidance: When creating or modifying an adventure, assume that the characters won't find all the magic items you place in it
overstocking_an_adventure.calculation: An adventure usually can include a number of items that's 25 percent higher than the number in the Magic Items Awarded by Level table (round up)
overstocking_an_adventure.example: An adventure designed to take characters from level 1 to 4 might include fourteen items rather than eleven, in the expectation that three items won't be found

magic_item_tracker.title: Magic Item Tracker
magic_item_tracker.description: You can use the Magic Item Tracker sheet to track how many magic items the characters have acquired
procedure: Each time the characters get a magic item, put a check mark in one of the empty circles corresponding to the item's rarity and the current level range
If the characters gain a magic item of a rarity that has no unchecked circles at the current level range, check off an empty circle from a lower tier
If all lower level ranges also have no circles left, check off an empty circle from a higher level range

random_magic_item_rarity.title: Random Magic Item Rarity
random_magic_item_rarity.overview: When you decide that a treasure contains magic items, there are two ways to determine the rarity of those items
methods: Choose an appropriate rarity based on the items you've given out already (using the Magic Item Tracker sheet to keep track)
Roll on the Magic Item Rarities table
random_magic_item_rarity.procedure: To use the table, find the level of the characters in the top row. Roll 1d100, and read down that column to find your roll. Then read across to the right column to find the rarity of the item

magic_items_for_starting_characters.title: Magic Items for Starting Characters
magic_items_for_starting_characters.overview: If you're starting a campaign for characters above level 1, Creating a Character offers suggestions for how many magic items such characters should start with and the rarity of those items

approaches.name: DM Choice
approaches.description: Choose items for each character using your own judgment

approaches.name: Player Choice
approaches.description: Let the players choose whatever items they want, within the specified rarity

activating_a_magic_item.title: Activating a Magic Item
activating_a_magic_item.overview: It usually takes a Magic action to activate a magic item. The item's user might also need to do something special
activating_a_magic_item.reference: The description of each item category or individual item details how an item is activated

command_word.title: Command Word
command_word.description: A command word is a word or short phrase that must be spoken or signed for an item to work
command_word.limitations: Spoken command words must be audible and fail to work in areas where all sound is suppressed, as in the area of the Silence spell

consumable_items.title: Consumable Items
consumable_items.description: Some items are consumed—used up, in other words—when they are activated
examples: A Potion of Healing must be swallowed
The writing vanishes from a scroll when it is read
consumable_items.result: Once used, a consumable item loses its magic

spells_cast_from_items.title: Spells Cast from Items
spells_cast_from_items.description: Some magic items allow the user to cast a spell from the item
mechanics: The spell is cast at the lowest possible spell and caster level
Doesn't expend any of the user's spell slots
Requires no components unless the item's description notes otherwise
The spell uses its normal casting time, range, and duration
The user of the item must concentrate if the spell requires Concentration
spells_cast_from_items.potion_bypass: Many items, such as Potions, bypass the casting of a spell and confer the spell's effects with its usual duration
spells_cast_from_items.exceptions: Certain items make exceptions to these rules, changing the casting time, duration, or other parts of a spell

spellcasting_ability.requirement: A magic item may require the user to use their own spellcasting ability when casting a spell from the item
spellcasting_ability.choice: If the user has more than one spellcasting ability, the user chooses which one to use with the item
spellcasting_ability.default: If the user doesn't have a spellcasting ability, their spellcasting ability modifier is +0 for the item, and the user's Proficiency Bonus applies

charges.title: Charges
charges.description: Some magic items have charges that must be expended to activate their properties
charges.identification: The number of charges an item has remaining is revealed when the Identify spell is cast on it
charges.attuned_knowledge: A creature attuned to an item knows how many charges the item has and how many it regains

the_next_dawn.title: "The Next Dawn"
the_next_dawn.description: Magic items often have charges or properties that recharge at the next dawn or some other specified time
the_next_dawn.dm_ruling: If such an item is on a world or plane of existence where the specified event doesn't occur, the DM determines when the item recharges

cursed_items.title: Cursed Items
cursed_items.description: A magic item's description specifies whether it bears a curse
cursed_items.detection: Most methods of identifying items, including the Identify spell, fail to reveal such a curse
attunement: Attunement to a cursed item can't be ended voluntarily unless the curse is broken first
Curses can be broken with a Remove Curse spell or similar magic

magic_item_resilience.title: Magic Item Resilience
magic_item_resilience.description: A magic item is at least as durable as a nonmagical item of its kind
magic_item_resilience.damage_resistance: Most magic items, other than Potions and Scrolls, have Resistance to all damage

artifacts.destruction: An Artifact can be destroyed only in some special way. Otherwise, it is impervious to damage
artifacts.knowledge: Learning how to destroy an Artifact usually requires research or the completion of a quest

crafting_magic_items.title: Crafting Magic Items
crafting_magic_items.overview: To create magic items, follow the rules below
crafting_magic_items.note: Equipment contains rules on brewing Potions of Healing and scribing Spell Scrolls
crafting_magic_items.scope: To create other magic items, follow the rules in this section

arcana_proficiency.title: Arcana Proficiency
arcana_proficiency.requirement: To craft a magic item, you and any assistants must have proficiency in the Arcana skill

rarity_tiers.tier_1_common_uncommon: Levels 1-4 (Tier 1) emphasizes Common and Uncommon items
rarity_tiers.tier_2_uncommon_rare: Levels 5-10 (Tier 2) emphasizes Uncommon items with increasing Rare
rarity_tiers.tier_3_rare_very_rare: Levels 11-16 (Tier 3) emphasizes Rare and Very Rare items
rarity_tiers.tier_4_very_rare_legendary: Levels 17-20 (Tier 4) emphasizes Very Rare and Legendary items
